topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The air in the room suddenly felt thick, suffocating. I walked in just as my roommate, Liam, was carefully turning a page in my spiral-bound journal. My breath hitched. I stopped dead in the doorway, my hands clenching into tight fists at my sides. The silence stretched, taut and brittle. Liam looked up, a faint flush creeping up his neck, and quickly snapped the book shut, sliding it under his mattress with unnecessary haste. "Whoa, what are you doing?" he asked, his voice pitched a little too high. I didn't move, just stared, the heat rising in my cheeks making my skin prickle. Every nerve ending felt hyper-alert.

[story 2]

It was the sight of my meticulous handwriting, my deeply personal anxieties spilled across the page, that made my stomach drop. I found him sitting at his desk, a faint glow from his laptop illuminating the open journal lying face-up beside his keyboard. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. When he finally glanced over, his eyes darted away instantly, a nervous tic flashing across his face. "Hey, sorry," he mumbled, picking up the book with a quick, jerky motion. "Just... looking." The way he avoided my gaze, the quickness of his retreat, sent a sharp, unpleasant jolt through me. I just stood there, my posture rigid, refusing to offer any acknowledgment of his clumsy intrusion.

[story 3]

The realization hit me like a physical blow when I noticed the smudge of ink on page forty-two—a page detailing a particularly vulnerable dream I’d written down weeks ago. I moved with a sudden, almost predatory stillness. I walked over, my footsteps unnaturally loud on the cheap carpet. Liam jumped, dropping his pen with a clatter. Seeing the incriminating evidence, his face went pale, and he started stammering apologies, his voice cracking with a genuine alarm. "I didn't mean to," he whispered, his eyes wide with distress. I just stood there, my gaze locked on the evidence, my shoulders squared, radiating a palpable sense of being deeply unsettled by his violation of my space.
